statement.markdown: Fix the bug in the provided CSS code to ensure that the `.header` and `.footer` classes are applied correctly to their respective HTML elements. The code should use the `!important` keyword to override any potential conflicts.
statement.inputFormat: A string containing the CSS code
statement.notes: The provided CSS code has a logical bug that needs to be fixed.

examples.explanation: The corrected CSS code uses the `!important` keyword to override any potential conflicts.

hints.text: Check for any potential CSS conflicts.
Use the `!important` keyword to override any conflicts.

editorial.title: Fixing CSS Class Name Conflicts
editorial.intuition: The provided CSS code has a logical bug that needs to be fixed. To fix the bug, we need to use the `!important` keyword to override any potential conflicts.
editorial.bruteForce: Try each possible combination of CSS rules to find the solution.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the `!important` keyword to override any potential conflicts.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The corrected CSS code should apply the `.header` and `.footer` classes correctly to their respective HTML elements.
editorial.dryRun: Test the corrected CSS code with different HTML elements to ensure it works as expected.
